RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that management:
Require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of all properties recorded in the books and submit a report thereon to the audit team;
Require the City Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain the property ledger cards and property records, respectively, as required by existing regulations; and
Reconcile the amount of actual properties counted with the recorded amount of properties in the books of the LGU and make the necessary adjustments.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the City Mayor create a Disposal Committee who shall take charge of the disposal of the unserviceable properties, following existing guidelines such as COA Circular No. 92-386 (Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments).

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the City conduct an inventory of its insurable properties, giving priority to high value items, retrieve the required building plans needed for filing insurance applications, appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Fund and insure the properties with the GIF of the GSIS.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that management desist from spending government funds for entertainment, which does not benefit the City Government but only a few private individuals and require the persons responsible for the irregular and unnecessary expenditure of ₱1,645,906.00 to immediately refund the amount disbursed upon receipt of the Notice of Disallowance to be issued by the audit team.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that management facilitate the immediate implementation of the PPAs funded by the National Government in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the implementation of the identified PPAs.
We further recommended that management require the City Accountant to monitor the fund releases from concerned NGAs through timely preparation of the required monthly bank reconciliation statements.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that management require the officials concerned to explain why there is a need to purchase an expensive Apple Macbook Pro and a high-end computer desktop with 26GB DDR3 Memory when the LGU could have purchased a cheaper laptop with the same specifications and a computer desktop with a standard memory, otherwise, they shall be required to restitute the amount used for the purchase of said luxurious IT equipment totaling ₱133,000.00.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that management strictly observe the limitations in the use of the SEF and to limit charges thereto to purposes allowed under Section 272 of the Local Government Code and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 1998.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommend that management demand from the treasurers of the various leagues of LGUs and local elective officials the acknowledgment of amounts paid to them with official receipts of the Republic of the Philippines (Accountable Form No. 51) in order that the funds will be disbursed for official purposes and subjected to government accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that management assign additional personnel for the sorting and arrangement of vouchers to be submitted to the auditor for post-audit and to monitor and ensure the timely preparation and submission of the required bank reconciliation statements, the monthly trial balances and transaction documents within the respective reglementary periods.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that the city discontinue the use of an open dumpsite and endeavor to operate a sanitary landfill as final disposal site for solid wastes.  This landfill should be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned under RA 9003.
We also recommended that the City Waste Management Board formulate specific guidelines on waste segregation at source and the establishment of collection systems for segregated solid waste in order to promote recycling and re-use of resources and to reduce the volume of waste for final disposal. Likewise, the City should strengthen their Information, Education and Communication (IEC) Program to increase the constituents’ awareness of waste management.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that the LDRRMF be reimbursed for the amount utilized for the purchase of expensive mobile phones, computer desktops and printers amounting to ₱216,010.00 and the officials concerned be required to restitute the amount used for the purchase of said luxurious cellular phones totaling P87,600.00.  Henceforth, limit the use of the LDRRMF to the purposes allowed by law and regulations.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that management:
Stop the hiring of JOs and charging their wages against appropriations for Clearing, Desilting, Declogging and Rehabilitation of Waterways which act is illegal and punishable by law;
Submit the required accomplishment reports of the concerned job order workers for the period January 1 to May 31, 2016; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 336 of the Local Government Code and Article VI, Section 29 of the Philippine Constitution which require that funds shall be used only for the specific purpose for which it was appropriated.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that management:
Stop the practice of charging against the LCPC Fund expenses which are not in consonance with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 on the proper utilization of the fund; and
Require the LCPC to perform their mandated functions and submit the required plans as enumerated under Section 3 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2008-126 dated August 21, 2008.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that management formulate a GAD plan which conforms with Joint Circular No. 2012-01 of the PCW, NEDA and DBM, to consider only those programs/activities/projects which address gender issues and to exclude the regular programs of the CSWDO and CHO in reporting accomplishments for GAD.

We recommended that management enforce settlement of cash advances by issuing final demand letters to concerned accountable officers and employees, after which impose the suspension of salaries of the erring accountable officers including the filing of appropriate administrative and criminal proceedings pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 8, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Unliquidated cash advances of retiring employees were deducted from the terminal leave benefit.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving the liquidation documents or loss of supporting documents required for liquidation of related cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management: 
a.	Submit the required accomplishment reports of the concerned job orders and corresponding certification from the LCE/Personnel Office on the necessity of hiring them to support the validity of subject disbursements totaling P48.60 million; and

b. Henceforth, require the submission of job orders’ accomplishment reports as proof that corresponding services have been rendered and indicate in the contract the specific job description or work to be performed by a job order worker.  Likewise, see to it that all payments for the first salary of job order workers are supported with the required certification that the activities/services rendered by them could not be provided by regular or permanent personnel of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Payments of wages were already supported with the required Accomplishment Reports and certification from the LCE on the necessity of hiring them.
observations.reason: Submission of accomplishment reports to support the 2015 job order payrolls is still ongoing.

observations.managementAction: Some of the projects were implemented during the year while some of the unexpended balances were reverted and appropriated for other development projects.
observations.reason: Problems in securing road right of way and insufficient funding of the programmed projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB see to it that funds allocated for SEF should be budgeted and utilized strictly in accordance with Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 and the rules and regulations issued under the DECS-DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A and 01-B to effectively attain the purpose for which the fund was created.
observations.managementAction: Traveling expenses of DepEd teachers were no longer charged to SEF Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a. 	Require Ms. Montecastro to refund the total amount of P122,799.28 representing the salaries and other compensation paid to her from January 1, 2015 to August 31, 2015, otherwise, the approving authority responsible for such illegal disbursements shall be personally liable for the total amount disbursed; and

b. 	Henceforth, follow strictly the pertinent provisions of IRR of R.A. No. 10154, as amended, on the responsibilities of employer-agency to a retiring government employee to avoid unlawful disbursement of government funds as well as administrative penalties for failure to conform to such responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: Staggered payment of disallowed amount is still ongoing.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, total refund amounted to ₱3,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe the prescribed minimum employee’s monthly net take home pay of ₱3,500.00 in CY 2016 pursuant to Section 52 of the General Provisions of the GAA of FY2016.
observations.managementAction: The prescribed minimum monthly take home pay is now being strictly observed by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the City Accountant to reconcile the account and remit whatever balance due to GSIS to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and enable the member-beneficiaries of the City to avail of benefits from the said office.
observations.managementAction: Remittances were reconciled with the billing statements every month. Also, a separate schedule is being maintained by the accounting office for their monitoring of collections and remittances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of charging expenses which are not in consonance with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73 on the guidelines for the utilization of 5% LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Repairs and Maintenance of CMO’s vehicle were no longer charged to LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Accountant to submit to the local COA auditor, through the LDRRM Office, LDRRM Council and LDC, copy furnished the OCD and DILG-LGOO the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of the same COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF are now regularly submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require all city officials and employees to refund the overpayment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2014 of P8,582,600.00 and henceforth, to observe strictly the limitations on the grant of PEI.
observations.managementAction: Refund through payroll deduction is still on going.
observations.reason: The city officials and employees have opted the installment method of payment of the disallowed PEI, hence total amount disallowed was not fully paid during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop immediately the payment of the employer’s share to the Pag-ibig Fund in excess of what is required by law and instead comply strictly with the prescribed rates effective CY 2015 and onwards.  Further, coordinate with Pag-ibig on the possible application of the overpayment of the government’s share in the succeeding monthly contributions.
observations.managementAction: The maximum monthly compensation of ₱5,000.00 is now observed in computing for the employer’s share contribution.
observations.reason: No action has been taken on the recommendation to coordinate with PAG-IBIG on the possible application of the overpayments of government’s share.

observations.recommendation: Require the City Accountant to stop immediately the grant of additional cash advances to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof has been made.
observations.managementAction: No additional cash advances were granted to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances that were granted from CY 2016 and onwards.
observations.reason: Unliquidated cash advances prior to CY 2016 were not considered in the granting of additional cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require all department heads to submit the Consolidated Requisition and Issue Slips for fuel on or before the 5th day of the following month to the General Services Office (GSO) and the latter to desist from approving requisitions for fuel unless the previous purchase has been fully liquidated.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of the consolidated RIS is still ongoing.
observations.reason: Due to time constraint RIS and the corresponding fuel consumption report was not yet submitted by the department heads to the GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the official concerned to refund the amount of P199,689.00. Moreover, management should consider restoring back the salary of the CDRRMO to the salary of a Project Development Officer II to avoid further disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The salary of the CDRRMO was restored back to the salary of Project Development Officer II.
observations.reason: Management has not collected the refund yet because the disallowed salary of CDRRMO is still under appeal with the Commission.

observations.recommendation: To protect the interest of the city, we recommended that management facilitate the transfer of the ownership of the 10,066 square meters of the lot purchased as soon as possible, otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Documents necessary for the transfer of title of lot are being processed by the concerned officers.
observations.reason: Several documents were already reproduced which were eventually lost and could not be found due to the death of the lawyer handling the documents.
